etymology.description: Triplication of 土(土) (tǔ, "earth; dirt") -> represents a tall mound or elevation of earth -> final form 垚
etymology.interpretation: The character 垚(yáo, "tall (of earth)") is formed by tripling 土(土) (tǔ, "earth; dirt"), visually depicting a mound of earth piled up to signify height or elevation. This visual repetition underscores its meaning of something being lofty when related to earth or soil.
